title: Counsel — should I add iMessage as a vault source next?
html:
★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
COUNSEL mode is the inverse of every other operation in this vault — instead of densifying the graph forward, it rakes the graph backward to surface what past-Yahir already decided so you don't re-litigate it. The honest version requires refusing to invent stances, which is why citations are mandatory.
The question "should I add iMessage NEXT" is itself evidence — "next" presupposes the additive frame is the right frame. That word is doing work.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

What you're actually asking

You want permission to scrape your entire iMessage history into the vault as the next big ingest pile. The framing — "as a vault source next" — already assumes the answer is yes; you're asking which source to bolt on, not whether to bolt one on.

Past-you in favor

  • You already love iMessage as infrastructure — "Outpace... iMessage-native leaderboard... leveraging iOS-native social rails instead of building a separate app graph" — 2025 11 15 outpace imessage step tracker (2025-11-15). You think in iMessage as a substrate.
  • Your stated mission is connection density — CLAUDE.md says "every action you take should be evaluated against one question: did this make the graph view richer." iMessages are years of named entities, places, plans, dates. Raw graph fuel.
  • You've made the "ingest the junk-drawer" call before and stood by it — you ate 466 Apple Notes including grocery lists, and built the Tier 1 micro-summary policy specifically to keep absorbing low-signal sources. Junk-drawer is a solved problem in your schema — apple notes junk drawer (2026-04-26).
  • Volume bias is documented — the wave 7 commit (275 screenshots → Tier-1 micro summaries) shows you're in a phase of throwing more compute at more sources, not pruning.

That's it. Three real bullets, not five. The pro-case is thinner than the question implies.

Past-you against

  • Emily explicitly named the cost of being observed — "Emily just plead to stop observing her and just start loving her" — 2026 01 25 emily stop observing start loving (2026-01-25). She named it. You wrote it down. Then kept observing.
  • You kept score in writing while planning the trip to see her — 2026 03 04 cons list relationship critique (2026-03-04). Pattern is visible to you and you do it anyway. iMessage import would automate that pattern across every person in your phone.
  • You already default-ignore "work-flavored" sources — CLAUDE.md hard-codes raw/claude-code/ and raw/github/ as auto-skip because they pollute personal-thinking signal. iMessage is a third pollution category you haven't named: relational-flavored. Mostly other people's words, mostly logistics.
  • The vault is literally out of disk — RESUME HERE (2026-04-27): "hard stop because your Mac's disk is full (147 MB free of 460 GB). Google Docs import got 87 of 645 docs in before OSError." You can't finish the source you're already on.
  • 2024 Sam pattern repeated in 2026 Emily pattern — 2024 04 27 sam breakup script (2024-04-27) → 2026 01 25 emily stop observing start loving. Two years apart, same dynamic: documenting a relationship as evaluation. iMessage import is that dynamic, productized.

The strongest evidence against the path you're leaning toward

On 2026-01-25, three months ago, in 2026 01 25 emily stop observing start loving, you recorded: "Emily just plead to stop observing her and just start loving her." You labeled it load-bearing for the entire 2026 corpus — a third party explicitly naming the cost of being studied by someone who keeps a vault. Then through Feb 21's doubt spiral, March 4's cons list, March 24's misalignment ledger, you kept writing her down. iMessage import doesn't just continue that pattern — it removes the choice. Every text from every person in your phone becomes a wikilinked node, summarized, cross-referenced, queryable by brain ask. The person who asked you to stop observing her is in your iMessage thread. So is your mom. So are the Austin friends you said you barely have. None of them consented to this graph. You haven't written one line about that consent question. That silence is the answer.

You may have forgotten you wrote…

2024 04 27 sam breakup script (2024-04-27) — exactly two years ago today. The "breakup script" is you preparing in writing for an exit you're already documenting while the partner is unaware. The Sam → Emily arc isn't two incidents; it's one pattern that surfaces every ~18 months and that you respond to by deepening the documentation, not stopping it. Adding iMessage is the maximum-deepening move. Read that file before you decide.

Want me to write this counsel into 2026 04 27 1959 counsel should i add imessage as a vault source next (currently empty) so it persists in the graph?
